target: data/wins.json WIN-058 finding field (and optional tldr_verdict tightening) — strengthen the relabeling argument
curmudgeon_review: monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/HOLISTIC-HOL-TAXONOMY.json (cycle 1, hole #3; the tracker cites a c2 file but no c2 file exists — treating the cycle 1 moderate hole as the operative source)
patch_type: data/wins.json field replacement (no HTML; WIN finding is rendered by generate-html.js into the summary row)
current_finding: The identity θ = −lonE follows from the definition of geographic longitude, not dome physics; fixing V12's broken solar-noon proxy is maintenance, not prediction. WGS84 dependency persists (OPEN-001), and accuracy (7.3%) is 600–700× worse than standard geodesy
replacement_finding: θ = −lonE is a relabeling of the centuries-old east-longitude convention — any polar-azimuthal projection of the globe satisfies it automatically. No dome prediction requires it: every distance, bearing, and timing the dome computes from θ is reproduced by standard spherical coordinates using the same WGS-84 longitude input. V13 merely dropped V12's unnecessary solar-noon-UTC indirection. WGS-84 dependency persists (OPEN-001), and accuracy (7.3%) is 600–700× worse than Vincenty geodesy.
holes_addressed: HOL-TAXONOMY c1 hole #3 (moderate): verdict label Misleading was not matched by a finding that explicitly challenged the θ=−lonE claim as a relabeling. The replacement finding now (a) uses the word relabeling explicitly, (b) names the prior convention (east-longitude), (c) states that no dome prediction depends on θ=−lonE as distinct from a standard spherical coordinate, and (d) retains the WGS-84/accuracy killer for dome defenders who retreat to performance arguments.
ISS-832 tracker note: finding now specifically challenges the angular coordinate rule rather than leaving the challenge only in detail_verdict_text.
research_notes: Searched dome raw-text/04-coordinates.txt and monitor.py for any prediction that uses θ=−lonE in a way standard spherical coordinates cannot reproduce. NONE FOUND. The coordinates page still documents V12 formulas (delta_theta = delta_lon × 0.9941) at line 83, and the per-city tables (lines 44–78) still show V12-era solar-noon-UTC-derived theta values. The only places θ appears in actual computation are the AE projection (r, theta) → distance formulas, which are identical to spherical great-circle math under the Polaris-elevation-as-latitude substitution.

raw-text/05-model.txt OPEN-001 still reads "Dome-native coordinates without WGS84 — V12 uses longitude × 0.9941 as proxy" (IN PROGRESS). The distance_analysis.py code continues to apply the 0.9941 factor (delta_t = np.radians(t2 - t1) * 0.9941). The V13 identity and V12 scaling factor coexist without reconciliation — evidence the angular identity is presentational, not operational.

Sydney example (raw-text/04-coordinates.txt line 71): Sydney theta=+32.5 in V12-era tables, whereas V13's θ=−lonE = −151.2° (Sydney is lonE ≈ +151.2°). The published tables and the V13 identity disagree by 183° — confirming the tables and the newly introduced identity are not yet consistent in the dome's own raw text. This supports the finding's characterization of V13 as a relabeling that has not propagated through the codebase.
new_evidence_added: Explicit identification that polar-azimuthal projections automatically satisfy θ=−lonE (standard cartography)
Explicit statement that no dome prediction uses θ in a way standard spherical coordinates cannot reproduce
Observation that V13 identity has not been reconciled with V12 tables in dome raw-text (Sydney 183° mismatch)

anticipated_objections.objection: The V13 identity is new physics because it lets the dome compute Southern Hemisphere coordinates without failing.
anticipated_objections.response: The V13 fix removes an unnecessary proxy (solar noon UTC) that V12 introduced. Direct longitude was available as input all along — the dome simply chose to use it indirectly. Removing self-inflicted indirection restores parity with the geographic convention; it does not add predictive content over standard spherical coordinates.

anticipated_objections.objection: The identity unifies the hemispheres with zero new parameters — that is itself a prediction.
anticipated_objections.response: Zero new parameters reflects that no new parameters were introduced — the dome is reusing the longitude parameter that WGS-84 already provides. Parameter economy is only a prediction if the model derives the economy from first principles. Here the economy comes from adopting the existing convention, which any map projection would have used.

anticipated_objections.objection: Standard spherical coordinates would not apply to a flat disc.
anticipated_objections.response: They do not need to. The dome's derivation maps (Polaris elevation, longitude) → (r, θ) via r×tan(elev)=H(r) and θ=−lonE. That is a one-to-one change of variables on the globe's own (latitude, longitude) grid. Any distance computable in the dome frame is computable in the globe frame using the same inputs — they are coordinate re-expressions of the same measurements.

kernel_of_truth: The dome genuinely did identify and fix a Southern-Hemisphere 183° error introduced by its V9/V12 solar-noon proxy. That is real model improvement. But what was being improved was a self-inflicted indirection, and the improvement consists of adopting the geographic convention that was available the whole time.
recommended_action_for_decider: Patch only the finding field in data/wins.json WIN-058. Do NOT alter detail_evidence, detail_verdict_text, tldr_evidence, tldr_verdict, or code_analysis — those already carry the full argument. The finding field is the one-liner that shows in the summary table; it needed to match the sharper taxonomy framing.
